notes: Parser-robustness sanity-check: a single transcript part is unusually large and the daemon ingests it without stalling, so the session still resolves ready→working→ready. No special opencode capability is exercised. INDUCTION: the small local model (lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored) won't reliably emit huge text on its own, so the recipe makes a TOOL produce the large part — turn1 asks opencode to write a large file (>256 KB of repeated bytes) in the run cwd via its Bash/write tool, turn2 asks it to read that file with its read tool, so the read-tool-result part becomes a single >256 KB row in opencode's SQLite store and a single >256 KB line in the driver-exported transcript.jsonl (one JSONL line per part). DAEMON PATH: opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter (agent.go:41) — the live watcher reads part.data straight from SQLite via rows.Scan (watcher.go:456-499) and ComputeMetrics likewise (metrics.go:67-103), with NO bufio line-buffer cap, so a single huge part is read whole. The shared tailer's readLineCapped 64 MB guard (tailer.go, issue #270) is the JSONL-adapter path and never gates opencode's store reads; the genuine >64 MB cap is pinned agent-agnostically by core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go. The phase DSL has no oversized-line form, so the resilience is asserted as user-observable state: the working→ready arc completing on the oversized-read turn IS the proof — had the read stalled, turn2_done would never fire. session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn1_start (matching working) establishes the ses_… session identity the later phases chain off. opencode's driver runs a blocking `opencode run` per send (not a held-open REPL), so the two sends are two turns on the same ses_… session (driver chains via --session); the cell asserts the arc and omits a separate teardown/process_exited phase like its opencode siblings. tool_calls_max:0 in the scenario verify refers to a permission GATE — the auto-executed file write/read needs no approval, so state never enters waiting.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch
PID bound to the ses_… session
First prompt (create the large file via a tool) drives the ses_… session ready → working
The file-creating tool auto-executes (no approval gate) and the turn completes → back to ready on the same ses_… session
Second prompt (read the large file back) drives the same ses_… session ready → working; the read-tool-result becomes a single oversized transcript part

The daemon ingests the oversized read-tool-result part without stalling and the turn completes → back to ready on the same ses_… session — arc completion is the parser-resilience proof; had the oversized part wedged the read path, this working→ready would never fire
